signal.raw_content:
WuXi STA Force Majeure Confirmed: What SM-A Intermediate Users Need to Know

Pharma Technology Focus — April 8, 2026

Following our breaking news alert on April 5, this article confirms details of the WuXi AppTec STA force majeure covering SM-A class protected amine intermediates.

CONFIRMED DETAILS (consistent with WuXi client notices dated April 4-5, 2026):

- Force majeure covers SM-A class protected amine intermediates (including the reference compound at CAS range 284462-xx-xx)
- Q2 2026 allocations cut by 40% from contracted quantities
- Q3 2026 allocation status under review; no committed quantities for Q3
- Return to normal supply: no earlier than Q1 2027 per WuXi's current assessment
- Cause: Building 7 fire (March 15, 2026) plus subsequent regulatory hold by Shanghai authorities

NO NEW INFORMATION: This article confirms and provides context for the force majeure announced April 4-5. WuXi has not provided updated allocation data, new supplier alternatives, or a revised timeline beyond what was communicated in the initial client notices.

ACTION ITEMS (unchanged from our April 5 bulletin):
- Audit current SM-A inventory
- Initiate emergency supplier qualification for alternative sources
- Notify regulatory affairs teams to begin NDA supplement planning
- Review drug shortage notification obligations with FDA

For updates, see our live supply chain disruption tracker.
